text.title: gemini-function-calling Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for gemini-function-calling. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Use manual function calling (enable_automatic_function_calling=False) in agent pipelines to allow logging, validation, and potential veto before execution.
rule.rationale: Automatic mode executes functions directly inside the SDK call. If a function raises an exception, the entire call fails with no retry opportunity. Manual mode gives the caller control and visibility.

rule.statement: Validate the function name in the response before executing: assert fc.name == expected_name.
rule.rationale: With tool_config mode="ANY", the model must call a function but may choose the wrong one. Validating the name prevents executing an unintended function.

rule.statement: Every tool function must have a type-annotated signature and a docstring that describes what the function does, its arguments, and its return value.
rule.rationale: Gemini parses Python docstrings and type hints to build the tool schema. Undocumented or unannotated functions may be ignored or called with wrong arguments.

rule.statement: Treat parallel function calls as non-deterministic in both order and selection. Always validate each call independently before executing.
rule.rationale: Gemini may call multiple tools in the same response in any order. Code that assumes a specific execution order will fail unpredictably.

rule.statement: Call dict() on fc.args explicitly, or use json_format.MessageToDict, before passing args to the function.
rule.rationale: fn_call.args returns a MapComposite protobuf object, not a plain Python dict. Passing it directly to **kwargs may silently omit fields or raise a TypeError.

rule.statement: Use Google Search grounding only when the task genuinely requires current web data — news, live prices, recent events.
rule.rationale: Grounding adds per-query cost and latency. For static knowledge, use the model's training data or a local RAG index instead.

rule.statement: Check grounding_metadata for source chunks after every grounded call and verify sources before surfacing them to users.
rule.rationale: Grounded responses cite web sources. Without verification, bad or irrelevant sources can undermine trust.

rule.statement: Do not combine Google Search grounding with custom tools in production — this combination is experimental and may behave unexpectedly.
rule.rationale: Mixing grounding and function calling is not fully supported on all Gemini models. The model may prefer grounding over tool calls in unspecified ways.

rule.statement: Use task_type="RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT" for indexing documents and task_type="RETRIEVAL_QUERY" for search queries.
rule.rationale: Embedding models use separate optimization heads per task type. Using the wrong type (e.g., RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT for a query) degrades retrieval quality measurably (10–20% lower recall).

rule.statement: Do not mix text-embedding-004 (768-dim) vectors with indexes built for 1536-dim (OpenAI) or 1024-dim embeddings.
rule.rationale: Dimension mismatch causes an error at index insert time or silently produces zero-similarity results at query time depending on the vector DB configuration.

rule.statement: Cache computed document embeddings — re-embedding on every request is expensive and the vectors do not change unless the document changes.
rule.rationale: Embedding API calls have cost and latency. Documents that have not changed produce identical vectors; there is no benefit to re-computing them.

rule.statement: Always normalize embeddings before cosine similarity computation using np.linalg.norm.
rule.rationale: Raw cosine similarity without normalization produces unstable results. Normalized vectors ensure consistent similarity scores across different batch sizes.

rule.statement: Always validate extracted JSON against the intended schema using Pydantic before using it downstream. response_mime_type="application/json" does not guarantee schema compliance — Gemini may return valid JSON that violates the schema.
rule.rationale: JSON mode produces valid JSON syntax, but may miss required fields or add extra fields. Pydantic validation catches these mismatches early.

rule.statement: Use manual function calling (not automatic) so the orchestrating agent can log, validate, and potentially veto tool invocations before execution. Pass the function response back using genai.protos.FunctionResponse.
rule.rationale: Manual mode gives the orchestrator full visibility and control. Automatic mode hides function execution and prevents the agent from reacting to execution failures.

rule.statement: Set a maximum iteration count on any agent loop using Gemini tools; the model can loop indefinitely on ambiguous tasks.
rule.rationale: Without a cap, a poorly phrased query can cause the model to call tools repeatedly without making progress. A sensible default is 10 iterations.
